viral_posts.hook: People think I don’t want to get married.
viral_posts.caption:
People think I don’t want to get married.

They’re wrong.

I want a wife who’s my best friend. Someone I can trust with my deepest fears, laugh with on the best days, and lean on during the worst ones. I want a peaceful home and a family built on loyalty, honesty, and commitment.

My standards are high.

Not because I’m looking for perfection, but because I’ve seen what happens when people choose the wrong partner.

The person you build a life with will influence your peace, your purpose, your children, your future… almost everything.

I’d rather wait a little longer for the right person than spend decades wishing I hadn’t settled.

Some decisions are simply too important to rush.

viral_posts.hook: You’re dating like you’re on a TV show. What happens when life gets really real?
viral_posts.caption: You’re dating like you’re on a TV show. What happens when life gets really real?

viral_posts.hook: God saw you getting ready to sacrifice everything so he saved you from that relationship.
viral_posts.caption: God saw you getting ready to sacrifice everything so he saved you from that relationship. That’s called mercy.

viral_posts.hook: Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath
viral_posts.caption:
Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You don’t trust happiness.

You trust struggle.

A lot of people don’t realize they’ve spent so much time surviving that peace starts to feel suspicious.

The reading says that good feelings can actually make us anxious.

Not because something is wrong.

Because something is right.

And if you’ve spent years carrying stress, chaos, heartbreak, or uncertainty, joy can feel unfamiliar.

So when life finally starts getting better, you start looking for what’s wrong with it.

Maybe happiness isn’t the thing leaving.

Maybe you’re just not letting it stay.

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together.

I love y’all.

Peace.

viral_posts.hook: “Gossiping about how I handled the worst year of my life instead of reaching out to see if
viral_posts.caption:
“Gossiping about how I handled the worst year of my life instead of reaching out to see if I’m okay is actually wild.”

I’ve been thinking about that a lot lately.

If you really believed I was struggling… why wasn’t your first instinct to call me?

Why wasn’t it, “Hey man, are you okay? Do you need anything?”

Instead, the people who knew me best just… disappeared.

Some of them talked about me instead of talking to me.

And that’s the part I can’t wrap my head around.

Because if I saw someone I loved going through the hardest season of their life, I wouldn’t make them a conversation.

I’d make sure they weren’t carrying it alone.

That experience taught me something I’ll never forget.

Some people don’t leave because you’re hard to love.

They leave because compassion was never their first instinct.

viral_posts.hook: If he wanted to, he would but make it for the boys
viral_posts.caption: If he wanted to, he would but make it for the boys

viral_posts.hook: Just because you’re attracted to someone doesn’t mean they’re meant for you.
viral_posts.caption:
Just because you’re attracted to someone doesn’t mean they’re meant for you.

Attraction can get you through the first date. It can’t carry a lifetime.

We’ve been taught to chase chemistry, but chemistry and chaos often wear the same disguise.

Instead of asking, “Do I want them?” try asking, “Who do I become when I’m with them?”

Do you feel peaceful? More yourself? More honest? Or do you feel like you have to earn their love?

You’re not just choosing someone to spend a Friday night with.

You’re choosing the person who might be sitting next to you on the worst day of your life.

Choose accordingly.

viral_posts.hook: The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until
viral_posts.caption: The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until they became other people.

viral_posts.hook: We don’t run to be fit, we run for the fit.
viral_posts.caption: We don’t run to be fit, we run for the fit.
